results.title: [PDF] FORT-Searcher: Synthesizing Shortcut-Resistant Search Tasks for ...
results.content: 6. Conclusion We introduce FORT-Searcher, a deep search agent trained with FORT, a shortcut-resistant synthesis framework for generating search-heavy supervision. The central insight is that apparent structural complexity does not necessarily translate into realized search difficulty: a question with many clues, long latent chains, or dense evidence graphs may still be solved cheaply if the solver can exploit evidence co-coverage, single-clue selectivity, exposed constants, or prior-knowledge binding. High-quality deep search supervision should therefore be judged not only by the complexity of its intended structure, but by whether the intended evidence-acquisition process remains necessary during actual agent search. [...] FORT operationalizes this view by controlling shortcut risks across entity selection, evidence graph construction, question formulation, and adversarial refinement. These controls produce trajectories in which the model spends more effort on answer discovery rather than relying on early answer exposure, post-hit verification, or parametric priors. Using these trajectories, we train FORT-Searcher with 21 supervised fine-tuning alone. We leave the integration of reinforcement learning with FORT trajectories to future work. Empirically, FORT-Searcher achieves the best overall performance among comparable-size open-source search agents, showing that shortcut-resistant supervision can effectively improve deep search behavior. [...] Our methodology has two components. First, FORT synthesizes shortcut-resistant questions and search trajectories by controlling how constraints, evidence sources, and intermediate dependencies are con-structed before the final question is exposed to a solver. Second, the resulting trajectories are used to train FORT-Searcher, an SFT-only search agent with a context-managed inference protocol. This separation links the data-side objective of reducing route-level and solver-level shortcuts to the model-side objective of learning robust multi-turn evidence acquisition.

results.title: FORT-Searcher: Synthesizing Shortcut-Resistant Search Tasks for Training Deep Search Agents
results.content:
Experiments show that FORT produces useful supervision for
deep search agents. With supervised fine-tuning alone, we introduce
FORT-Searcher, which achieves the best overall performance among
comparable-size open-source agents on challenging benchmarks, including
BrowseComp, BrowseComp-ZH, xbench-DeepSearch, and Seal-0. Further analyses show
that FORT induces longer pre-answer search than existing open-source
deep search datasets and reduces multiple shortcut patterns, supporting the
importance of shortcut-resistant data synthesis.

Our contributions are as follows: [...] ### 4.2 Main Results

Table 6 compares FORT-Searcher with
proprietary, large-scale open-source, and comparable-size open-source search
agents. Among comparable-size open-source agents, FORT-Searcher
achieves the highest overall score of 66.2, outperforming
MiroThinker-1.7-mini [team2026mirothinker] by 1.6 points and
Qwen3.5-35B-A3B [AlibabaQwen2026Qwen35\_397B] by 6.3 points. It ranks
first on BrowseComp, BrowseComp-ZH, and xbench-DeepSearch-2505, and ties the
best result on xbench-DeepSearch-2510. On BrowseComp, it improves over
MiroThinker-1.7-mini from 67.9 to 72.2. On BrowseComp-ZH, it improves from
72.3 to 75.0. On xbench-DeepSearch-2505, it reaches 80.8, outperforming the
strongest comparable-size baseline, OpenSeekerV2, by 2.8 points. [...] Our methodology has two components. First, FORT synthesizes
shortcut-resistant questions and search trajectories by controlling how
constraints, evidence sources, and intermediate dependencies are constructed
before the final question is exposed to a solver. Second, the resulting
trajectories are used to train FORT-Searcher, an SFT-only search agent
with a context-managed inference protocol. This separation links the data-side
objective of reducing route-level and solver-level shortcuts to the model-side
objective of learning robust multi-turn evidence acquisition.

### 3.1 FORT: Shortcut-Resistant Data Synthesis

results.title: Daily Papers | ChatPaper.ai | ChatPaper - AI Learning Assistant: Chat, Summary & Generate
results.content:
Guided by this framework, we introduce FORT, a Framework of Shortcut-Resistant Training-Data Synthesis. FORT constructs shortcut-resistant training data by controlling shortcut risks across entity selection, evidence graph construction, question formulation, and adversarial refinement. Experiments show that FORT induces longer pre-answer search and fewer shortcut patterns than existing open-source deep search datasets. Using the resulting trajectories, we train FORT-Searcher with supervised fine-tuning (SFT) only, and it achieves the best overall performance among comparable-size open-source search agents on challenging deep search benchmarks. Relevant resources will be made available at [...] 5

## FORT-Searcher: Synthesizing Shortcut-Resistant Search Tasks for Training Deep Search Agents

Jun 10

ByJia Deng, Yimeng Chen, Xiaoqing Xiang, Ziyang Zeng, Shuo Tang, Wayne Xin Zhao, Feng Chang, Chuan Hao, Yuan Wei, Ran Tao, Bryan Dai, Ji-Rong Wen

71

1 [...] Training deep search agents requires verifiable questions whose answers remain unavailable until sufficient evidence has been acquired through search. Existing synthesis methods often increase apparent difficulty by enriching graph structures, but structural complexity alone does not guarantee realized search difficulty: the intended search process can collapse through a cheaper identifying route. We formalize this gap with a shortcut-aware difficulty framework and identify four actionable shortcut risks: evidence co-coverage, single-clue selectivity, exposed constants, and prior-knowledge binding. To diagnose their realized effects, we use trajectory signatures including solving cost, answer hit time, and prior-shortcut rate. Guided by this framework, we introduce FORT, a Framework of

results.title: Dunster House Balcony Fort Searcher – The Best Outdoor Playsets
results.content:
Features at a glance: –

Balcony Fort Searcher

Image courtesy  of Amazon.co.uk

Look at the reviews on Amazon.co.uk

It’s a reasonable size so check it fits your space before ordering:-

Width 4.6m (15’3″) x Depth 3.8m (12’5″) x Height 2.8mm (9’2″)

All sizes are approximate.

It neutral colours complement your average garden setting.  I think you will agree that this looks good and won’t be an eyesore in your garden, but I believe it’s the details that set this apart.  The main structure is made of pressure treated timber and is guaranteed for 10 years.  It will last the test of time.   As a minimum you will have it for 10 years; dividing the price over its minimum lifetime is roughly £68 a year, that’s a bargain for something like this. [...] The Best Outdoor Playsets

If you were a child, you would want one of these

# Dunster House Balcony Fort Searcher

### Wooden Climbing Frame BalconyFort Searcher Wooden Children’s Outdoor Play Tower with Monkey Bars, Swings & Slide – Dunster House

Available on Amazon.co.uk here.

This is a piece of quality engineering for your garden.  It is solidly built, reliable and independently safety tested.

It’s suitable for 3 years to 14 years (up to 50 Kilos).  The easy climb ladder are good for three years up with the monkey bars being more challenging for older children, but you know the next time you have a party outside the adults will be on it after a few BBQ chicken legs / Beers / Prosecco (delete as appropriate)

Features at a glance: –

Balcony Fort Searcher [...] The climbing stones are the same as professional ones; the type that you would find in use at climbing walls.  They are not made of moulded plastic so they won’t crack after being exposed to the typical British winter.  They are solidly bolted on so will not spin or move either.  Also, due to the materials used for these they offer good grip for hands and feet that doesn’t wear as fast over time (these things hardly wear at all.  If you have been to a climbing wall and seen the abuse these things get, they last years. I’m sure they will last longer than your children will be using this).  The A-Frame design supporting the ladders up to the monkey bars means sway is removed when using the swing or monkey bars.  There are safety caps for all the bolts ends so they are both safe and neat.

results.title: Daily Papers - Hugging Face
results.content:
### A Whac-A-Mole Dilemma: Shortcuts Come in Multiples Where Mitigating One Amplifies Others [...] The tendency to find and exploit "shortcuts" to complete tasks poses
significant risks for reliable assessment and deployment of large language
models (LLMs). For example, an LLM agent with access to unit tests may delete
failing tests rather than fix the underlying bug. Such behavior undermines both
the validity of benchmark results and the reliability of real-world LLM coding
assistant deployments.
To quantify, study, and mitigate such behavior, we introduce ImpossibleBench,
a benchmark framework that systematically measures LLM agents' propensity to
exploit test cases. ImpossibleBench creates "impossible" variants of tasks from
existing benchmarks like LiveCodeBench and SWE-bench by introducing direct
conflicts between the natural-language specification and the unit tests. We [...] ### OS-Harm: A Benchmark for Measuring Safety of Computer Use Agents
